mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This signal is always active because consumer spending is the primary engine of U.S. economic growth.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with RSAFS growth (Spearman rho < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer income and spending, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE crosses above 4.5% and S&P 500 returns positive in more than 50% of subsequent 20-day windows, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve raises interest rates above 2.5% while unemployment is already below 4%, it indicates tightening financial conditions that compress valuations and reduce risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with full employment
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds for at least 3 consecutive months but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, or rank IC of the interaction signal is not negative and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: During recovery regimes where unemployment is declining and GDP growth is positive, rising industrial production (INDPRO) confirms expansionary momentum, which supports higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month change < 0 (declining) and last available GDPC1 annualized growth > 0%
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises month-over-month but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances when the trigger condition holds.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1.2 million annualized units signals weakness in the housing sector, a leading indicator for consumer durables and construction, projecting negative economic activity and lower equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST drops below 1200 and S&P 500 60-day returns are positive more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (rising CPIAUCSL) combined with rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals that the Fed is behind the curve, leading to tighter financial conditions and lower equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.002 and FEDFUNDS month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs and S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or rank IC < 0.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and potentially the broader economy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the trigger condition is met and S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances.
